text: The contrast, ‘Abraham was but one, and we are many’ appears somewhat forced. It seems just possible that this paragraph is addressed, not to Jews, but the heathen still living in Palestine, who were not descended from Abraham, and here express their intention of disputing his inheritance. It is perhaps worth noticing that the sin of eating meat with the blood in it (verse 25) is not elsewhere mentioned among the delinquencies of Israel.
Literally, ‘they turn them (thy words) into a song of their mouth’. The Hebrew text seems to mean ‘they make loves in their mouths’, but for ‘loves’ the Septuagint Greek has ‘lies’.
